title: Understanding Position Vectors
text: A position vector describes the position of a point in space relative to an origin. It is expressed in terms of unit vectors along the coordinate axes.

title: Unit Vectors in 3D
text: In a 3D coordinate system, the unit vectors are \(\hat{i}\), \(\hat{j}\), and \(\hat{k}\), representing the $$x$$, $$y$$, and $$z$$ axes, respectively.

title: Forming the Position Vector
text: To form the position vector, multiply each coordinate by its corresponding unit vector and sum them up.

title: Calculate the Position Vector
text: Given the coordinates $$(1.0$$, $$-4.0$$, $$6.0)$$, calculate the position vector using the unit vectors \(\hat{i}\), \(\hat{j}\), and \(\hat{k}\).
